subject: Add 'controller-m3' project
commit_message:
Add 'controller-m3' project

This replaces Drools code with equivalent Java code, as a step
towards completely non-Drools control loop processing.

Update: use PolicyEngine thread pool instead of ForkJoinPool

Updates to address merge conflicts, but no other comments were addressed:
- bumped pom version
- handle duplicate events (i.e., same info but different request ID)

Fixed breakage due to actor property name/structure changes.
Fixed a couple of eclipse warnings about missing serialization ID.
Fixed issues from new checkstyle.

Issue-ID: POLICY-2532
Change-Id: Ia9cf9c0dd7df84ba00f35647f2ad1fd47a98ac1b
Signed-off-by: Straubs, Ralph (rs8887) <rs8887@att.com>
Signed-off-by: Jim Hahn <jrh3@att.com>

Patch Set 1: Verified-1

Build Failed 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-applications-master-verify-java/1471/ : FAILURE

Unable to build due to a non-resolvable parent POM. Search logs for "[FATAL] Non-resolvable parent POM" to find the logs.

The fix for this is usually to make sure your poms are pointing to parents that exist. Double check:

 * groupId is correct and no typos
 * artifactId is correct and no typos
 * version is correct for branch ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-applications-master-verify-java/1471/ )

There are test failures. Please look in the log to identify them. ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-applications-master-verify-java/1471/ )

It is saying that it can't find 'org.onap.oparent:oparent:pom:3.0.0', which is several levels up the project hierarchy -- I don't see how this change could cause that error. I am also seeing some test failures due to timeouts that I don't see in my environment.

There is no way this work is acceptable at all.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-2

> There is no way this work is acceptable at all.

Clearly, the build failure needs to be resolved, and I am working on trying to track that down. Is that the sole reason for your verdict, or is there something more?

Patch Set 2:

1) We have not branched yet for Guilin
2) Your team has left other work unfinished - namely everything called 'm2'. That needs to be resolved first before we keep piling up technical debt.
3) Let's stop this habit of naming things 'm3' - that makes no sense to ONAP.
4) Let's fix the JIRA - add in accurate description of this so the code changes I see here actually match.
5) Even before the JIRA - the ONAP community needs to be made aware of this feature being added. Community acceptance. It touches code that others utilize.

Patch Set 2:

(7 comments)

Need individual junits for the two new classes.  I didn't bother making sonar-related comments.
